title: 10 best herbal capsules for dry cough
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for dry cough are natural supplements designed to alleviate the discomfort and irritation caused by dry coughs. They work by soothing the throat and reducing inflammation, providing relief from the persistent coughing and congestion. The best herbal capsules for dry cough include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Echinacea purpurea, Zingiber officinale, Thymus vulgaris, Eucalyptus globulus, Ephedra sinica, Ginkgo biloba, Mentha x piperita, Astragalus membranaceus, and Verbascum thapsus. If left untreated, dry cough can lead to fatigue, loss of sleep, and decreased productivity, severely impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with dry cough because they contain glycyrrhizin, a natural compound that soothes and calms irritated throats. The capsule's mucilages help to coat the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry coughs. Additionally, licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce congestion and irritation in the respiratory tract, providing relief from persistent coughing. As a result, herbal licorice capsules provide effective and natural relief for dry cough symptoms.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and irritation in the throat, which can contribute to a dry cough.
Flavonoids: Have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the airways and calm the cough reflex, providing relief from a dry cough.
Saponins: Act as expectorants, helping to thin and loosen mucus in the lungs, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion that can contribute to a dry cough.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps with dry cough by reducing inflammation and soothing the mucous membranes in the throat, thereby relieving dryness and irritation.
Flavonoids: Specifically, Quercetin, a flavonoid present in Glycyrrhiza glabra, has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce coughing and soothe the respiratory tract.
Saponins: Saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra have expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, thereby relieving dry cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary source of glycyrrhizin, a compound responsible for its medicinal properties and cough-relieving effects.
Rhyzomes: Similar to roots, they contain glycyrrhizin, which helps in soothing dry coughs and providing relief.
Leaves: Although less commonly used, leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra contain glycosides, which contribute to their medicinal properties and cough-relieving effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the fall season after the plant has completed its growth cycle.
Clean the roots by gently scrubbing them with a soft brush under cold running water for 5 minutes.
Dry the roots by placing them in a single layer on paper towels for 2 weeks.
Grind 1 part dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots with 1 part excipients into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Fill empty capsules with 250 mg of the powdered glycyrrhiza glabra mixture and seal them tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with dry cough because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the irritated throat and chest, providing fast relief from annoying symptoms. The menthol in peppermint has a cooling effect that loosens mucus and reduces inflammation, making it easier to expel phlegm and relieve congestion. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce the irritation and discomfort associated with dry cough, allowing for more comfortable breathing and a better night's sleep.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol has natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, providing relief from dry coughs.
Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol has anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties, which help to reduce swelling in the throat and nasal passages, easing congestion and coughing.

Volatile Oils: The volatile oils present in peppermint capsules have a soothing effect on the mucous membranes, calming irritation and inflammation in the throat and airways, providing relief from dry coughs and sore throats.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps to thin mucus and relieve congestion, making it easier to cough up and providing temporary relief from dry cough.
Limonene: Exhibits expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, thereby reducing dry cough symptoms.
Rosmarinic acid: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, thereby relieving dry cough.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain high concentrations of menthol and menthone, which have expectorant and decongestant properties to help relieve dry cough.
Flowers: The flowers have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe the throat and reduce coughing.
Stems: The stems contain menthol and other essential oils that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh mentha x piperita leaves when in full bloom for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 105°f for 2 hours to prevent spoilage.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder for uniform texture.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the ground mentha x piperita powder with 500 mg of gelatin or vegetable capsules.
Fill the capsules with the herbal mixture and seal them using a capsule filler machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsules helps with dry cough because they contain the natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of thyme, which soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat and lungs. The capsules work to thin out mucus, making it easier to expel, while also reducing inflammation and irritation caused by dry air and environmental allergens. As a result, herbal thyme capsules can provide quick relief from dry cough symptoms, offering a natural and effective solution for temporary respiratory discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antitussive: Thyme contains compounds like thymol, which helps to soothe and calm coughing by reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract, making it less irritating and allowing for easier expectoration of mucus.
Expectorant: The volatile oils present in thyme, such as cineole, help to loosen and clear out excess mucus and phlegm from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.

Antiviral and antibacterial: Thyme's antimicrobial properties can help to combat infections that may be contributing to the dry cough, such as viral or bacterial bronchitis or pneumonia, by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and viruses.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol is a phenolic compound that helps with dry cough by acting as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, thereby relieving cough symptoms.

Carvacrol: Carvacrol is a monoterpenic phenol that helps with dry cough by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, reducing the growth of pathogens that can exacerbate cough symptoms, and by its anti-inflammatory effects, which help to soothe the respiratory tract.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid is a phenolic diterpene that helps with dry cough by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to the development of a dry cough.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain thymol, a compound that helps in relieving cough and has antimicrobial properties.
Stems: They also contain thymol, which contributes to their use in treating dry cough and other respiratory issues.
Flowers: They have a higher concentration of thymol and other volatile oils that aid in soothing a dry cough and providing relief.
remedy_recipe: Dry 200g of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Crush the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 10g of the powdered thymus vulgaris leaves with 50g of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal the capsules immediately.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with dry cough because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the irritated mucous membranes in the throat. The capsules contain a combination of antioxidants and vitamins that help to reduce inflammation, making it easier to expectorate mucus and relieve congestion. Additionally, dandelion's diuretic properties can help to thin out mucus, making it easier to cough up and providing relief from persistent dryness and irritation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and irritation in the respiratory tract, alleviating dry cough symptoms.
Mucilage-rich: The capsule's mucilage content coats the throat and lungs, soothing and protecting them from further irritation, reducing coughing fits and discomfort.

Expectorant: Dandelion's expectorant properties help loosen and expel mucus and phlegm from the airways, relieving congestion and making it easier to clear out debris that may be contributing to dry cough.
remedy_constituents: Inulin: Inulin, a type of fructan, helps with dry cough by reducing inflammation and soothing the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract.
Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol, a triterpenoid saponin, exhibits anti-inflammatory properties that aid in relieving dry cough by reducing swelling and congestion in the airways.
Taraxasterol acetate: Taraxasterol acetate, another triterpenoid saponin, has expectorant properties that help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, providing relief from dry cough.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high mucilage content, which helps to soothe and calm the respiratory tract.
Roots: Utilized for their bitter compounds and saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and relieve cough symptoms.
Flowers: Employed for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing congestion and soothing the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 fresh taraxacum officinale roots and clean them thoroughly with a soft brush.
Dry the roots in a single layer at 50-60 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours.
Grind 1 gram of dried taraxacum officinale root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powder with 500 milligrams of gelatin in a small bowl.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 1-2 grams of the taraxacum officinale gelatin mixture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with dry cough because they contain mucilages, which are natural soothing agents that coat and protect the mucous membranes in the throat. When taken orally, these capsules release a gentle film of mucilage that lubricates and calms irritated tissues, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry coughs. The soothing properties also help to thin mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion, allowing for a more effective and efficient recovery from a dry cough.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow root contains a thick, protective mucilage that helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by dry cough.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The flavonoids and saponins present in marshmallow root have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and airways, alleviating symptoms of a dry cough.

Soilution and lubrication: Marshmallow root's mucilage helps to solubilize and lubricate the mucus in the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion, which can contribute to the persistence of a dry cough.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages in Althaea officinalis help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat, thereby reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by dry cough.
Icterin: Icterin, a flavonoid glycoside, has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate dry cough symptoms.

Gallic acid: Gallic acid, a phenolic compound, exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help combat infection and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, thereby alleviating dry cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain mucilages, which help soothe and calm the respiratory tract, providing relief from dry cough.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate dry cough.

Flowers: The flowers are used because they contain mucilages and flavonoids, which combine to provide soothing and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to calm and heal the respiratory tract and relieve dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of althaea officinalis roots from a clean and dry environment.
Clean the roots by rinsing them with distilled water and pat dry with paper towels.
Grind 50g of the cleaned roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1g of the powder with 10g of gelatin and 5g of glycerin to form a uniform blend.
Fill 100 empty capsules with the uniform blend using a capsule filler machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein capsules helps with dry cough because they contain a natural anti-inflammatory agent that soothes and calms irritated airways, reducing coughing fits. The herb's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus, easing congestion and coughs. Additionally, mullein has been shown to have antitussive properties, helping to reduce the frequency and severity of dry, hacking coughs. By targeting these underlying causes, herbal mullein capsules provide natural relief from dry, persistent coughing.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Mullein contains mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes, providing relief from dry, scratchy throats and reducing inflammation.

Saponins: The saponin content in mullein capsules has natural expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear out mucus, bronchial secretions, and phlegm, allowing for easier expulsion and alleviating congestion and coughing.

Antioxidants: Mullein's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to chronic dry coughs by reducing the body's natural defense mechanisms and protecting against cellular damage.
remedy_constituents: Verbascoside: This iridoid glycoside has mucilaginous properties, which can help soothe and calm the irritated mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, providing relief from dry cough.

Verbascosidic acid: A phenylethanoid glycoside, verbascosidic acid has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and promote the expulsion of mucus, alleviating dry cough symptoms.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin): As a powerful antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, quercetin can help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate dry cough by promoting mucus secretion and improving lung function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their high mucilage content, which helps to soothe and calm dry coughs.
Stems: The stems are used for their similar mucilage content to leaves, providing relief from dry coughs and irritation in the throat.
Seeds: The seeds are also used due to their ability to help reduce inflammation and provide relief from dry coughs, although their use is less common compared to leaves and stems.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried flowering tops of the plant containing the medicinal properties.
Dry the harvested plant material in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the powdered plant material with 2 parts of gelatin to create the capsule mixture.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them tightly using a capsule sealer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with dry cough because they contain a unique compound called podophyllotoxin, which has natural expectorant properties. This compound helps to loosen and clear out mucus from the airways, providing relief from persistent dry coughs. Additionally, the capsules also possess anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the irritated mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry cough.
remedy_properties: Antitussive effect: The capsule's flavonoids, particularly quercetin, have a soothing and calming action on the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and relieving dry cough symptoms.

Expectorant properties: Ginkgo biloba's terpenes, such as sesquiterpene lactones, help loosen and remove excess mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and alleviating congestion associated with dry coughs.
Anti-inflammatory activity: The capsule's flavonoids and terpenes also have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the lungs and airways, which can contribute to dry cough relief.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and soothe the mucous membranes, thereby alleviating dry cough symptoms.
Bilobalide: This triterpenoid helps to reduce inflammation and protect the bronchial tubes from irritation, which in turn can help to alleviate dry cough.
Quercetin: A type of flavonoid, quercetin is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the airways and alleviate dry cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves are the most commonly used part for making capsules to relieve dry cough due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Leaves: The leaves are also rich in glycosides, which help to thin mucus and make it easier to cough up, providing relief from dry cough symptoms.
Leaves: In addition, the leaves of Ginkgo biloba contain antioxidants that help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in late summer or early fall when they are fully ripe and have turned yellow.
Dry the ginkgo biloba leaves in a warm place with good air circulation for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mill or grinder.
Mix 500-1000 mg of the ginkgo biloba powder with 10-20% magnesium stearate and 5-10% silicon dioxide.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the ginkgo biloba mixture and seal immediately to preserve the herb's potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with dry cough because of their soothing properties, which can ease inflammation in the throat and airways. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds present in lavender help to calm irritated tissues, reducing the severity of a dry cough. Additionally, the capsules' calming effects on the nervous system can also promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep despite persistent coughing. As a result, English lavender capsules can provide natural relief from dry cough symptoms, helping to improve overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, which can alleviate symptoms of dry cough.

Expectorant properties: The camphor and borneol present in lavender capsules help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to expel, thereby relieving congestion and dryness associated with a dry cough.

Anxiolytic and calming effects: Lavender's relaxing and soothing properties can help reduce anxiety and stress, which are common triggers of dry coughs, and promote relaxation, allowing for improved sleep and reduced symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that helps to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to dry cough, and has a relaxing effect on the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Linalyl acetate: A terpene that has anti-inflammatory and mucolytic properties, helping to break down and clear mucus from the airways, reducing the severity of dry cough.
Luteolin: A flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to dry cough.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Lavandula angustifolia leaves are used to make capsules for dry cough because they contain volatile oils and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.

Flowers: Lavandula angustifolia flowers are used to make capsules for dry cough because they contain a high concentration of essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which have soothing and calming effects on the respiratory system.
Seeds: Lavandula angustifolia seeds are not typically used to make capsules for dry cough, however Lavandula angustifolia seeds are not mentioned in the list of most used parts.
remedy_recipe: Dry 50g of lavandula angustifolia flowers in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind 25g of the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 10g of the powder with 2g of gelatin and 5ml of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the mixture, using 0.2g per capsule.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese date capsules helps with dry cough because they contain a unique blend of herbs that soothe and calm the respiratory tract. The capsules' key ingredients, such as licorice root and mulberry leaf, work together to reduce inflammation and ease congestion, allowing for easier breathing and relief from persistent dry coughing. Additionally, the capsules' natural expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus, providing rapid and effective relief from coughs caused by dry air or allergies.
remedy_properties: Antitussive: The capsules contain ingredients like dates, licorice root, and honey, which have anti-tussive properties that help soothe and calm a dry cough, reducing its frequency and severity.

Mucilages: The mucilage content in the capsules, particularly from plant sources like dates and slippery elm, helps to moisten and protect the mucous membranes, alleviating inflammation and irritation caused by dry coughs.

Expectorant: The herbal ingredients in the capsules, such as ephedra and menthol, help to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel and relieve congestion and coughing associated with a dry cough.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help with dry cough by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate cough symptoms.
Saponins: Saponins in Ziziphus jujuba help with dry cough by acting as expectorants, which can loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.

Terpenoids: Terpenoids in Ziziphus jujuba have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response in the respiratory tract, thereby alleviating dry cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Ziziphus jujuba fruits are used to make capsules for dry cough because they contain antitussive and expectorant properties that help relieve coughing.
Seeds: Ziziphus jujuba seeds are used to make capsules for dry cough because they have anti-inflammatory and antitussive properties that help soothe the throat and reduce coughing.

Leaves: Ziziphus jujuba leaves are used to make capsules for dry cough because they possess antitussive and antimicrobial properties that help reduce inflammation and fight infections in the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ziziphus jujuba fruit at ripeness in late summer to early fall when the fruit is fully red.
Dry the harvested fruit in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 24 hours to reduce moisture.
Grind 500 grams of dried fruit into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 1 minute.
Mix the powder with a binding agent such as gelatin in a ratio of 1 part gelatin to 5 parts powder.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filling machine to create herbal capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony capsules helps with dry cough because they contain the extract of Peonia lactiflora, a natural herb that has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries. The capsules help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, relieving congestion and inflammation that can exacerbate dry coughs. By reducing inflammation and promoting expectoration, Chinese peony capsules can help to break up mucus and provide quick relief from dry, hacking coughs. Regular use of these capsules may also help to reduce the severity and frequency of dry cough episodes over time.
remedy_properties: Antitussive property: Chinese Peony capsules have antitussive properties, which helps to relieve and reduce the frequency of dry coughing by suppressing the cough reflex.

Expectorant property: The capsules have expectorant properties, which helps to loosen and remove phlegm and mucus from the lungs and airways, relieving congestion and discomfort associated with dry coughs.

Anti-inflammatory property: Chinese Peony capsules have anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, thereby alleviating irritation and discomfort caused by dry coughs.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to reduce inflammation in the airways and loosen mucus, thereby relieving dry cough.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Paeonia lactiflora have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe and protect the respiratory tract, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with dry cough.

Saponins: Saponins in Paeonia lactiflora have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and loosen mucus in the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving dry cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help relieve dry cough.
Stems: They contain compounds that aid in reducing inflammation and congestion, contributing to their use in dry cough remedies.
Leaves: They are used because of their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm irritated respiratory tracts.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried paeonia lactiflora roots and flowers in the fall season when the plant is mature.
Dry the harvested plant material at 40°c for 2 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind 250g of the dried paeonia lactiflora into a fine powder using a grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 100g of the powdered paeonia lactiflora with 500mg of gelatin in a bowl and let it sit for 30 minutes.
Encapsulate the gelatin mixture into 100 capsules using a capsule machine with a capacity of 500 capsules per hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary capsules helps with dry cough because of its natural expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs. The herb's essential oils, such as camphor and borneol, have anti-inflammatory effects that soothe the throat and reduce irritation caused by dry air or allergens. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as carnosic acid, help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate the discomfort associated with dry cough.

Expectorant properties: Rosemary's expectorant compounds, such as camphor and borneol, help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing relief from congestion and coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated throats.
Leaves: Fresh or dried rosemary leaves contain essential oils that ease respiratory congestion and reduce the severity of dry coughs.
Roots: Rosemary roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated throats.
Barks: The bark of rosemary contains flavonoids that act as natural expectorants, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves from a trusted source or store-bought quantity.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 50g of the ground leaves with 500ml of boiling water to create an herbal infusion for 20 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding solids after 10 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250mg of the infused liquid and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with dry cough because they contain natural expectorants that help loosen and clear out mucus from the airways, providing quick relief from congestion. The herbs in elder capsules also have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the throat and reduce irritation, making it easier to breathe and speak comfortably. Additionally, elder capsules' antiviral and antibacterial compounds may help combat infections that can cause dry coughs, further reducing symptoms and promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Elderberry's antiviral properties help to reduce the severity and duration of a dry cough by inhibiting the replication of viruses such as the common cold and flu, thereby alleviating symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in elder capsules, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, which can contribute to a dry cough and make it more uncomfortable.

Mucilaginous properties: Elderberry's mucilage content helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat and airways, providing relief from dryness and irritation that can accompany a dry cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots are rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm a dry, irritated cough.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in late summer or early fall when they are in full bloom.
Dry the sambucus nigra plant material in a warm oven at 60°c for 24 hours or until brittle to preserve its medicinal properties.
Grind the dried sambucus nigra into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle to increase its surface area.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powdered sambucus nigra with 200 milliliters of water in a small bowl to create a smooth paste that can be filled into capsules.
Fill gelatin capsules with the prepared sambucus nigra paste and seal them immediately to preserve their potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with dry cough because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, has been shown to reduce inflammation in the lungs and throat, providing relief from dry cough symptoms. Additionally, turmeric's expectorant properties help loosen mucus, making it easier to expel, while also soothing the throat and reducing irritation. As a result, turmeric capsules can provide effective natural relief from dry coughs, allowing for a more comfortable night's sleep and improved overall health.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the airways, alleviating dry cough symptoms caused by respiratory issues such as bronchitis or asthma.

Antimicrobial properties: Turmeric contains various compounds with antimicrobial effects, which can help combat infections causing dry coughs by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and viruses responsible for these conditions.
Expectorant properties: Turmeric has natural expectorant properties that can help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, providing relief from dry cough and congestion.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that helps relieve dry cough symptoms by reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.
Roots: Containing bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have natural expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs.
remedy_recipe: Measure accurately 500mg of dried turmeric powder from curcuma longa root.
Add 250mg of powdered ginger and 125mg of licorice root powder to the mixture.
Mix the powders thoroughly for at least 30 seconds to ensure uniform blend.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with the blended powder using a capsule filler machine.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container protected from light and moisture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with dry cough because they contain a unique combination of compounds that work together to soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat, relieving dryness and irritation. The active ingredients in fennel capsules, such as flavonoids and saponins, help to reduce inflammation and increase mucus production, making it easier to expel and clear out the respiratory tract, providing effective relief from persistent dry coughs.

Seeds: Containing a natural anti-inflammatory compound, they soothe the throat and reduce swelling, making them effective in calming dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves in the morning when dew is still present on the leaves for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a shaded area or using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours to remove excess moisture.
Crush 50 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves into fine powder using a mortar and pestle to enhance extraction efficiency.
Mix the powdered foeniculum vulgare with 100 grams of gelatin capsules in a bowl, filling each capsule with approximately 250 milligrams of the herbal mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to six months or refrigerate for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena capsules helps with dry cough because of its natural soothing properties, which help to calm and quiet the irritated airways. The herbal extract contains citral and limonene compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing congestion and inflammation in the lungs and throat. Additionally, lemon verbena's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, providing relief from stubborn coughs and promoting a smoother breathing experience.
remedy_properties: Antitussive and expectorant properties: Lemon verbena's active compounds, such as citral and geraniol, have been shown to reduce cough frequency and severity by loosening mucus and reducing inflammation in the airways.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenes present in lemon verbena capsules may help reduce inflammation in the lungs and airways, which can exacerbate dry cough symptoms such as irritation and discomfort.

Antioxidant and soothing properties: Lemon verbena's antioxidant and soothing compounds may help calm irritated mucous membranes, reduce oxidative stress, and promote overall respiratory health, thereby helping to alleviate dry cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and congestion associated with dry cough.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and terpenes that help soothe and calm the respiratory tract, providing relief from dry, scratchy coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers of known identity and purity.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 60 degrees fahrenheit for 24 hours.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder to ensure uniform texture.
Mix 1 part powdered verbena officinalis with 2 parts carrier medium such as gelatin capsules or silica dioxide and fill into #0 vegetarian capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months and consume 3-4 capsules twice daily with water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silverleaf capsules helps with dry cough because of its unique ability to soothe and calm the respiratory tract. The herb's natural expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, reducing inflammation and congestion that can exacerbate a dry cough. Additionally, silverleaf capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce irritation and discomfort in the throat and lungs, providing fast and effective relief from dry, hacking coughs. As a result, many people find that taking silverleaf capsules helps to quiet their cough and promote a more restful night's sleep.
remedy_properties: Expectorant Properties: Herbal Silverleaf Capsules contain saponins, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieve congestion.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The capsules' active compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, alleviating dryness and discomfort associated with a dry cough.

Mucilage Formation: The mucilages present in Herbal Silverleaf Capsules help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by dry coughs.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots help alleviate congestion and soothe irritated airways.
Rhyzomes: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, rhyzomes effectively reduce swelling and relieve discomfort associated with dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried cistus x incanus leaves and flowers in amounts of 20 grams per capsule.
Grind the cistus x incanus mixture into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 15 minutes.
Combine 200mg of ground cistus x incanus with 100mg of excipient material such as silica or calcium carbonate.
Fill #00 gelatin capsules with the prepared herbal blend using an encapsulation machine within 10 seconds.
Store the finished capsules in a cool dark place for up to six months to maintain potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum capsules helps with dry cough because they contain the natural properties of Eucalyptus globulus, a plant native to Tasmania. The capsules release a soothing and expectorant-rich oil that loosens and clears mucus from the lungs and airways, providing relief from persistent dry coughs. Additionally, the antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties of Tasmanian blue gum help reduce inflammation in the throat and chest, further alleviating discomfort and congestion associated with dry coughs.
remedy_properties: Expectorant Properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's essential oil acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, relieving congestion and making it easier to cough up phlegm.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: The capsules' anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to a dry cough by reducing swelling and irritation in the airways.
Antimicrobial Properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's antimicrobial properties may help combat bacterial and viral infections that can cause or exacerbate a dry cough, such as bronchitis or pneumonia.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated airways.
Barks: Contains tannins that aid in reducing the production of mucus and relieving cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality eucalyptus globulus leaves and wash them thoroughly in cold running water.
Dry the eucalyptus leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6 hours or until crispy.
Grind dried eucalyptus leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500mg of ground eucalyptus powder with 100mg of organic rice flour and fill capsules using a capsule machine.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milkweed capsules helps with dry cough because they contain compounds that have natural expectorant properties. The capsules stimulate the production of mucus in the lungs, which helps to loosen and clear out congestion, providing relief from dry, hacking coughs. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of milkweed help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing discomfort associated with a dry cough.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Saponins have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the airways, loosen mucus, and ease congestion, making it easier to expel phlegm and soothe a dry cough.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory actions, which can help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by irritants, further reducing coughing fits and discomfort.

Alkaloids: Alkaloids, such as lappaconitine and delphinine, have antispasmodic properties that can relax the smooth muscle in the airways, reducing constriction and spasms that often exacerbate dry coughs.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated throats.
Seeds: Contain mucilage, a thick, protective substance that coats and protects the throat, reducing irritation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality asclepias syriaca leaves and flowers in the early morning ensuring maximum potency and freshness.
Dry the plant material using shade drying or oven drying at low heat for 24 hours to preserve nutrients.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder for optimal extraction.
Mix 500mg of the powdered asclepias syriaca with 250mg of tapioca starch and fill capsules using a capsule machine within 30 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or freeze for longer preservation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with dry cough because it contains compounds that have a soothing effect on the respiratory system, reducing inflammation and relieving irritation in the throat. The herb's natural anti-inflammatory properties help to calm the cough reflex, making it easier for the body to expel excess mucus and phlegm, thereby providing relief from dry, scratchy throats. Additionally, valerian's calming effects can also promote relaxation and reduce stress, which are known triggers of coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Valerian's sedative effects can help to calm the body and promote relaxation, which can help reduce the severity of dry cough symptoms by decreasing irritation in the throat.

Expectorant properties: Valerian's expectorant properties can help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion and discomfort associated with dry cough.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian's anti-inflammatory effects can help to reduce inflammation in the throat and lungs, which can exacerbate dry cough symptoms and make them more difficult to manage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in volatile oils and sesquiterpenes, the roots are often used to create a soothing expectorant effect that helps loosen mucus in the chest, relieving symptoms of dry cough.

Rhyzomes: The rhizome's high concentration of valepotriates, a type of glycoside, has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in reducing coughing spasms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valerian root at dawn or before noon to ensure optimal potency.
Cleanse the harvested roots by washing them thoroughly with cool water and mild soap.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm well-ventilated area for 7-10 days or until completely brittle.
Crush the dried valerian root into small pieces using a mortar and pestle then sift through a fine-mesh sieve to ensure uniform size.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with approximately 500mg of crushed valeriana officinalis powder per capsule and seal.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry cough that are treated with herbal capsules are bronchitis, asthma, and pharyngitis. These conditions often manifest as recurring or persistent coughs, making herbal supplements an effective natural remedy. Capsules containing herbs like Thyme, Goldenseal, Echinacea, and Slippery Elm help soothe the respiratory tract, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms such as congestion and wheezing.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with dry cough is a blend of Thyme, Echinacea, and Licorice root. Thyme's antibacterial properties help combat underlying infections, while Echinacea boosts the immune system to fight off the cause of the cough. Licorice root soothes the throat and reduces inflammation, providing quick relief from dry, irritated tissues. This combination works synergistically to alleviate dry cough symptoms, promoting a faster recovery and overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with dry cough because of its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The active compound, glycyrrhizin, soothes and calms the irritated throat and bronchial passages, reducing inflammation and discomfort. It also helps to thin mucus, making it easier to cough up, thereby providing quick relief from dry, persistent coughs. The natural and gentle properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules make them an effective and safe remedy for dry coughs, providing rapid relief and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Licopyrone: It helps to soothe and calm the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to dry cough.
Glycyrrhizin: It has anti-inflammatory properties and helps to reduce the severity of cough by relaxing the muscles in the respiratory tract and reducing mucus production.
Flavonoids: They have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the respiratory tract, thereby alleviating dry cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that helps relieve dry cough.
Leaves: The leaves have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the throat and reduce the severity of dry cough.
Barks: The barks of the plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, helping to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can contribute to dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a reputable source to ensure quality.
Grind 300g of the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the powder with 1 tablespoon of cornstarch in a small bowl.
Fill 500 empty gelatin capsules with the glycyrrhiza glabra mixture using a capsule filler.
Store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea capsules helps with dry cough because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, alleviating dry cough symptoms. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea is rich in antioxidants, which help to protect the body from oxidative stress and promote overall health. By supporting the immune system and reducing inflammation, Echinacea purpurea capsules can provide relief from dry cough and promote a speedy recovery. Regular consumption may also help to prevent future infections.
remedy_constituents: Isoquinoline alkaloids: They help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the airways, relieving dry cough symptoms by modulating the immune system's response to pathogens.
Cichoric acid: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, reducing dry cough and associated discomfort.
Caffeic acid: This phenolic acid has antioxidant properties that help to protect the respiratory system from oxidative stress, reducing inflammation and alleviating dry cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of alkaloids, which have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties.
Leaves: They are used for their bioactive compounds, such as polyphenols and flavonoids, which possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Flowers: They are used for their high content of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and immune-stimulating effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of echinacea purpurea roots and 50g of flowers from a well-drained soil area 3-4 weeks after blooming.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place at 35-40°c for 2-3 days to reduce moisture content to 10%.
Grind the dried echinacea purpurea roots and flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 10g of the echinacea powder with 50g of gelatin capsules in a bowl and fill each capsule individually with 500mg of the mixture.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with dry cough because they contain the natural properties of ginger, which possess anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion. Additionally, the expectorant properties aid in thinning and loosening mucus, making it simpler to cough out, providing relief from dry cough. This natural remedy can offer a soothing and effective solution for those suffering from dry cough.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, providing relief from dry cough.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols also possess anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the throat, reducing the urge to cough.
Zingiberene: This terpene has expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and providing relief from dry cough.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part for making capsules for dry cough because they contain the highest concentration of active compounds such as gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Roots: Roots are another essential part used for making capsules due to their rich content of gingerols and other bioactive compounds that help to relieve cough symptoms and reduce inflammation.

Stems: Stems, although less commonly used, are sometimes utilized for making capsules due to their ability to provide a more sustained release of bioactive compounds compared to rhyzomes or roots, which can help to prolong the relief from dry cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested rhizomes in a single layer at 50c for 2 days to remove moisture.
Grind 50g of dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a high-speed mill for 30 seconds.
Mix the powder with 100g of vegetable cellulose to create a uniform blend in a container.
Fill 100 empty capsules with 0.5g each of the zingiber officinale powder blend using a capsule filler.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris capsules helps with dry cough because they contain the essence of thyme, a natural expectorant that loosens mucus and reduces inflammation in the respiratory tract. The active compounds in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have antimicrobial properties that combat infection and soothe the throat. By reducing congestion and killing bacteria, thymus vulgaris capsules provide quick relief from dry coughs, making them a popular natural remedy for respiratory issues. Their anti-inflammatory properties also promote healing and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Acts as an expectorant to help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, reducing the severity of dry cough.
Caryophyllene: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviate dry cough symptoms.
Rosmarinic acid: Possesses antioxidant properties, which help protect the respiratory system from oxidative stress and inflammation, thereby reducing the occurrence of dry cough.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of thymol, a compound that helps reduce cough and congestion.
Buds: Used because they contain a high concentration of essential oils, which are known for their decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Used as they contain a significant amount of thymol and other essential oils that help alleviate dry cough symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves at dawn when the dew is still present.
Dry the thymus vulgaris leaves in a single layer at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried thymus vulgaris leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the thymus vulgaris powder with 5 capsules of gelatin for 30 seconds.
Fill 100 capsules with the thymus vulgaris mixture using a capsule filling machine in 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus capsules helps with dry cough because they contain the essential oil eucalyptol, which has natural expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. When inhaled, the oil helps loosen and clear mucus from the airways, reducing congestion and relieving coughs. The anti-inflammatory properties also help soothe and calm irritated respiratory tissues, providing quick and effective relief from dry cough symptoms. As a result, eucalyptus globulus capsules can be a valuable natural remedy for people suffering from dry coughs and related respiratory issues.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): It helps to thin and loosen mucus, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion associated with dry cough.
Cascarillan: It has expectorant properties, helping to bring up mucus from the lungs and airways, thus providing relief from dry cough.
α-Pinene: It has anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, which help to reduce inflammation and open up airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving dry cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are widely used due to their high eucalyptol content, which provides expectorant and decongestant properties to relieve dry cough.
Barks: The bark is rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds, contributing to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe a dry cough.
Stems: The stems of Eucalyptus globulus contain eucalyptol and other volatile oils, which aid in reducing inflammation and providing relief from dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25 grams of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves from well-managed forests or cultivated fields.
Dry the eucalyptus leaves at 60-70 degrees celsius for 2 hours using a food dehydrator.
Grind 15 grams of dried eucalyptus leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 500 mg of eucalyptus powder with 500 mg of gelatin or vegetarian capsule filler in a small bowl.
Fill 100-200 vegetarian capsules with the eucalyptus-gelatin mixture using a capsule filling machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ephedra sinica capsules helps with dry cough because they contain ephedrine alkaloids, which are natural expectorants that relieve congestion and break down mucus. The herb's natural decongestant properties help to thin and clear out mucus, making it easier to cough up. Additionally, Ephedra sinica has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the throat and reduce irritation, allowing for a more effective and comfortable coughing process. This natural remedy can provide quick relief from dry cough symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Ephedrine: Acts as a bronchodilator, helping to relax airway muscles and increase airflow, thereby relieving dry cough symptoms.
Norephedrine: Exhibits expectorant properties, helping to thin mucus and promote its clearance from the airways, which can help alleviate dry cough.
Tetrahydroprotoberberine: Displays antimicrobial properties, which may help combat underlying infections that can contribute to dry cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Ephedra sinica contain ephedrine, which helps to relieve dry cough by acting as a bronchodilator.
Seeds: Ephedra sinica seeds are also a rich source of ephedrine, making them a suitable ingredient for cough capsules.
Stems: The stems of Ephedra sinica contain ephedrine and other bioactive compounds that help to alleviate dry cough symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with dry cough because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The leaves of the Ginkgo biloba plant contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which work together to soothe and calm the respiratory tract. This helps to reduce inflammation and irritation in the throat, providing quick relief from dry coughs. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus, making it easier to cough up and alleviate congestion. This makes Ginkgo biloba a popular natural remedy for dry cough.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the lungs, making it easier to clear mucus and alleviate dry cough symptoms.
Bilobalide: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the respiratory tract and reduce irritation, thereby alleviating dry cough and promoting easier mucus clearance.
Ginkgolides: These diterpenes have anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic effects that help relax the airways and reduce coughing, making it easier to manage dry cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Seeds: The seeds are also used due to their potential in reducing inflammation and coughing, although they may not be as commonly used as the leaves.
Barks: Some sources may use the barks due to their potential in providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in late autumn or early spring when leaves are fully developed.
Dry ginkgo biloba leaves in a low-temperature oven at 100°f for 2-3 hours or use a dehydrator.
Grind dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder or coffee grinder.
Mix 1-2 tablespoons of ginkgo biloba powder with 1 tablespoon of gelatin or starch in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin or starch capsules with the ginkgo biloba mixture using a capsule filler and seal tight.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita capsules helps with dry cough because they contain peppermint oil, a natural expectorant that loosens and clears mucus from the lungs and airways. The menthol in peppermint oil also has a soothing effect on the throat, reducing irritation and inflammation that can exacerbate dry cough. Additionally, peppermint oil's decongestant properties help to open up airways, making it easier to breathe and relieving the discomfort associated with dry cough. This natural remedy provides fast and effective relief.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Acts as an expectorant, helping to thin mucus and relieve congestion associated with dry cough.
Linalool: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe the throat and reduce coughing.
Menthone: Possesses decongestant and expectorant properties, helping to clear mucus from the airways and alleviate dry cough symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are widely used due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which help in relieving cough and congestion.
Stems: Stems, along with leaves, contain essential oils that contribute to their decongestant and expectorant properties, making them effective in treating dry cough.
Buds: The buds of Mentha x piperita, rich in menthol and other volatile compounds, are used to create cough relief capsules due to their soothing and expectorant effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha x piperita leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated for 1-2 hours.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer at 30-40°c for 2-3 hours in a food dehydrator.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder for 1 minute.
Mix the powdered mentha x piperita with a small amount of vegetable cellulose in a 1:1 ratio by weight for 2 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus capsules help with dry cough because they contain potent anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties that aid in soothing the throat and reducing congestion. The herb's adaptogenic properties also help to boost the body's natural defenses, which can help to alleviate the underlying causes of dry cough. By supporting the immune system and reducing inflammation, Astragalus membranaceus capsules can provide relief from dry cough and promote overall respiratory health. Regular use may also help to prevent future cough episodes.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds help reduce inflammation in the airways, thereby alleviating dry cough symptoms.
Flavonoids: By exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, flavonoids in Astragalus membranaceus capsules help soothe the throat and lungs, providing relief from dry cough.
Terpenoids: These compounds possess expectorant properties, which can help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the airways, thus alleviating dry cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Astragalus membranaceus are commonly used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, which are effective in treating respiratory issues such as dry cough.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of Astragalus membranaceus are used because they contain a high amount of astragalosides, which help to soothe and calm the respiratory system, reducing dry cough symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves of Astragalus membranaceus are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties and help to alleviate dry cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of astragalus membranaceus roots with a diameter of 1-2 inches in the fall.
Dry the roots in a dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 8-12 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powder with 100 milligrams of silica as a filler.
Encapsulate the mixture into 100-200 vegetarian capsules using a capsule machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus capsules helps with dry cough because of their expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the airways. The active compounds in Verbascum thapsus, such as verbascoside and isoverbascoside, have anti-inflammatory and mucolytic effects, reducing congestion and making it easier to cough up mucus. This can provide relief from dry, hacking coughs and help alleviate respiratory discomfort, promoting overall respiratory health and well-being. Regular use of Verbascum thapsus capsules may also help soothe irritated airways and promote a healthier respiratory system.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These constituents help with dry cough by exhibiting expectorant properties, which aid in loosening and expelling mucus from the respiratory tract.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Verbascum thapsus have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the airways, thereby alleviating dry cough symptoms.
Saponins: Saponins in these capsules possess soothing properties that help calm the irritated airways, providing relief from dry cough.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are commonly used to make capsules for dry cough due to their high mucilage content, which helps soothe and calm the respiratory tract.
Leaves: Leaves are another key part used in dry cough remedies due to their high mucilage content, which provides relief from cough and congestion.
Flowers: Flowers of Verbascum thapsus are used to make capsules for dry cough due to their expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Collect 25g of dried verbascum thapsus flowers and leaves from a trusted source for medicinal use.
Grind the collected material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Sift the powder through a 100-mesh sieve to remove any lumps and debris from the mixture.
Mix 1g of the sifted powder with 2g of gelatin and 5ml of distilled water to create a uniform solution.
Fill a size 00 empty capsule shell with the prepared mixture and seal it immediately to preserve freshness.
